title: Reply to: Experiments implementing small commuting models lack gravitational features
data_sources: Classical numerical simulations of SYK Hamiltonian
Google Sycamore quantum processor (original experiment, ref. 2)

data_scope.computational_results: Simulations of thermalization, operator size winding, and perturbed Hamiltonian behavior
data_scope.quantum_system: Small-N Majorana fermion SYK model (near-term quantum hardware scale)

core_conclusion: Holographic duality in the SYK wormhole experiment applies process-specifically to the two-sided teleportation protocol, not to the entire Hamiltonian. The SYK Hamiltonian is not 'gravitational' for all processes, but the targeted two-sided teleportation has a valid traversable wormhole interpretation because the relevant low-temperature correlation functions are dominated by the Schwarzian mode.
why_it_matters: Clarifies the scope of quantum gravity analogues on near-term quantum hardware; defends process-specific holography; uses thermalization and size winding as experimental diagnostics; advances the machine-learned Hamiltonian paradigm for quantum simulation.
why_nature: High-profile Matters Arising reply in a cornerstone Nature debate; provides foundational theoretical clarification on process-specific holography; bridges quantum information, condensed matter, and high-energy physics; connects Google Quantum AI hardware experiments to theoretical physics.
added_value_to_my_research: Process-specific interpretation principle -> identifies which spatial interaction processes follow gravity-law vs. local heterogeneity in 2SFCA catchment delineation
Learned Hamiltonian + holographic validation -> physics-informed learned spatial interaction kernels validated against gravity-law exponents from GPS data
Size winding / operator growth diagnostics -> spatiotemporal information-spreading metrics on urban graphs for detecting cascade effects and diffusion anomalies
RL-for-process-discovery in quantum simulation -> RL to find which trip-type sub-processes are gravity-approximable in EV charging and facility-location optimization
Schwarzian-mode physics-informed constraint -> encoding spatial statistical laws as inductive biases in graph neural operators for spatiotemporal SIR and KDE models
